description: The figure presents the calculated relationship between the logarithm of the equilibrium constant logK_AlN (solid lines) and the logarithm of the actual concentration product logQ_AlN (dashed lines) as a function of solidification fraction z_s for three Fe-15Mn-xAl-C low-density steels. Black, blue, and red curves correspond to samples 1# (Fe-15Mn-1Al-C), 2# (Fe-15Mn-3Al-C), and 3# (Fe-15Mn-5Al-C), respectively. The intersections of the solid and dashed curves mark the solidification fractions at which AlN inclusions precipitate, yielding values of 0.46 for 1#, 0.22 for 2#, and 0.51 for 3#. The data reveal a non-monotonic trend: increasing Al content from 1% to 3% advances AlN precipitation (fraction decreases from 0.46 to 0.22), whereas further increasing Al to 5% delays precipitation (fraction rises to 0.51). This behavior reflects the combined effects of Al and N solute segregation and the shift in solidification route with varying Al content, supporting the conclusion that Al addition non-monotonically governs the precipitation sequence of AlN inclusions during solidification.
